news.title: Bharat Future City draws 90 real estate proposals, approvals crawl amid delays
news.para: Real estate developers are cautiously investing in Bharat Future City's development. Most investment activity is concentrated along specific highways, and many await a clearer roadmap. The Future City Development Authority has received numerous applications, but only a few have secured approvals. Developers are adopting a wait-and-watch approach until the master plan is ready. Staff shortages at the FCDA are reportedly causing delays in processing applications.

news.title: Volvo Group leases six lakh sq ft office space in Bengaluru
news.para: India's office market recorded its strongest-ever quarter in Q2 2026, with gross leasing reaching a record 24.6 million sq ft, according to CBRE. Volvo Group signed one of the quarter's largest deals, leasing 600,000 sq ft in Bengaluru, as GCCs, flexible workspaces and technology firms continued to drive demand despite geopolitical uncertainty.

news.title: Rooms in stilt parking razed, guesthouses sealed in Gurugram DLF-3
news.para: Gurugram authorities demolished illegal structures in stilt parking areas.  Four properties were sealed for unauthorized commercial activities in residential buildings.  Encroachments were cleared from approximately 4.5 kilometers of public right of way.  This enforcement drive followed previous actions and complied with court directives.  The campaign will now proceed to DLF-4 for further action.

news.title: Telangana HC grants relief to Doolapally homeowners over Fox Sagar FTL row
news.para: The Telangana High Court has ordered the state government to refrain from coercive actions against Doolapally homeowners. This directive came after homeowners challenged government orders revoking their construction permissions. Petitioners argued that their properties were outside the Fox Sagar lake's Full Tank Level. They presented official maps showing their lands were not within the buffer zone. The court granted interim protection pending further hearings on July 27.

news.title: Alterations allowed in Delhi development body flats, with riders
news.para: Delhi Development Authority revised its housing policy after many years.  Owners of upper-floor flats can now build permanent structures.  This move aims to provide additional usable space while maintaining uniformity.  Construction must not obstruct common areas or interfere with utility services.  The revised policy simplifies regularisation and ensures building safety.

news.title: CM hands over key to first flat under PMAY-Urban in Ghaziabad
news.para: Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath handed over the first Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban units. This event marked the initial handover of homes since the scheme's announcement. Delays were caused by funding and infrastructure issues, which have now been addressed. The Chief Minister also inaugurated and laid foundation stones for ninety projects. These projects are collectively worth eight hundred sixty-eight crore rupees.

news.title: Cyberabad EOW books builder for cheating homebuyers in ₹24 crore flat project
news.para: Cyberabad EOW registered a case against Pranith Koncepts and its directors.  The firm allegedly collected crores from homebuyers for a Puppalaguda project.  Developers failed to deliver flats and secure mandatory project approvals.  Buyers paid substantial amounts but received no possession or registration.  Investigation is ongoing as evidence is being collected by officials.

news.title: CBI files chargesheet against Ozone Urbana in homebuyers' fraud case
news.para: The CBI filed its 17th charge sheet in the scam before the Special Court in Bengaluru against the builder for "alleged fraudulent activities" relating to a housing project situated in the city, they said.

news.title: Single-family housing starts dips in June; building permits lowest in 10 months in USA
news.para: U.S. single-family homebuilding declined for a third month in June. Future construction permits dropped to a ten-month low due to higher mortgage rates. Builders face constraints from rising land and material costs. Housing starts for projects with five or more units saw a significant increase. Manufacturing output remained unchanged, while factory production grew robustly.

news.title: Oberoi Realty’s net profit rises 29% to ₹543.51 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Oberoi Realty’s total income increased 26.8 per cent to ₹1,361.69 crore during the quarter. Its board has also declared an interim dividend of ₹2 per equity share for FY27.

news.title: Havells India’s net profit declines 17% to ₹289.71 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Havells India’s consolidated total income increased 19 per cent to ₹6,572.35 crore during the quarter. Revenue from its lighting and fixtures segment rose to ₹400.62 crore.

news.title: Notandas Realty directors acquire Juhu bungalow Villa Wilson for ₹135 crore
news.para: The property is spread over a 1,038.50-sq-metre plot and is located near Amitabh Bachchan’s bungalow Jalsa. It is the directors’ second bungalow acquisition in Juhu in 2026.

news.title: Civic body sets up tax camps as Panchkula property tax recovery lags
news.para: Panchkula Municipal Corporation is organizing property tax camps to assist residents with dues.  These camps aim to help defaulters clear outstanding amounts before the extended August 31 deadline.  A 75% interest waiver is available on arrears if paid by August 31, 2026.  Over 60% of properties have not yet completed self-certification for tax purposes.  Failure to comply may lead to recovery proceedings and legal action against property owners.

news.title: Upgradation, allotment of over 7,400 EWS flats at Bhalswa being expedited: Delhi minister
news.para: Delhi Urban Development Minister Ashish Sood announced the expedited upgradation of over 7,400 EWS flats.  Existing flats are being expanded to improve living conditions for beneficiaries.  The government plans phased allotment after completing the repair and expansion work.  This initiative aims to provide dignified housing for economically weaker families.  The project budget has significantly increased under the current administration.

news.title: Ranchi cracks down on illegal rural construction
news.para: Ranchi administration warns property owners about illegal rural construction. Mandatory building plan approvals are required for constructions over 5,000 square feet. The Zila Parishad now handles layout plan approvals for rural areas. Owners must submit digital applications through registered architects for transparency. Failure to comply risks legal action and demolition liabilities.

news.title: HC allows Bengaluru development body to invite applications for Karanth layout but bars site allotments
news.para: The Karnataka High Court modified its earlier order regarding the Dr K Shivaram Karanth Layout.  The Bangalore Development Authority can now invite applications for sites.  However, allotments require prior court permission, which is a key restriction.  This modification addresses the authority's mounting interest burden on project loans.  The court considered the BDA's financial position and loan pressures.

news.title: ITC Hotels net profit up 36% at ₹181.91 crore; to acquire GHK Hospitality & Infrastructures
news.para: ITC Hotels reported a thirty-six percent profit increase for the first quarter.  Consolidated revenue from operations also saw a rise compared to the previous year.  The company announced the acquisition of GHK Hospitality & Infrastructures Ltd for one hundred fifty-five crore.  This acquisition will expand ITC Hotels' owned-asset portfolio in Ahmedabad.  The hotel business revenue grew while the new residences vertical also contributed.

news.title: Odisha government has begun appointing a new member to RERA
news.para: The Odisha government has begun appointing a new member to the Real Estate Regulatory Authority. This appointment follows the earlier departure of the previous administration member. The chairperson's post at the Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal also remains vacant. Stakeholders express concerns about delays affecting dispute resolution processes. Timely appointments are crucial for consumer protection and sector confidence.

news.title: Over 10,600 Ahmedabad civic body housing units found unsafe, redevelopment begins
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is redeveloping over 10,600 unsafe housing units. This initiative follows a structural survey of aging civic body-owned residential buildings. Redevelopment has begun for nearly 8,000 units, with more awaiting resident consent. The South Zone has the highest concentration of dangerous AMC housing. This urgent drive addresses recent monsoon-related building collapses across the city.

news.title: Builder, partner booked for duping retiree of ₹12.58 crore over a decade in Pune
news.para: Pune police registered a cheating case against a builder and his partner.  They allegedly defrauded an 80-year-old man of Rs 12.58 crore over ten years.  The builder failed to deliver six flats after taking Rs 11 crore investment.  Later, they promised three shops but never delivered possession of those.  The elderly man approached police after finding no construction work had started.
